allow: Bash(bun -e "const p = Bun.spawn\([''which'', ''claude''], { stdout: ''pipe'', stderr: ''pipe'' }\); const t = await new Response\(p.stdout\).text\(\); const e = await new Response\(p.stderr\).text\(\); console.log\(''stdout:'', t\); console.log\(''stderr:'', e\); console.log\(''exit:'', await p.exited\)")

Bash(git -C /Users/jon/@luca add src/agi/features/assistant.ts src/agi/container.server.ts src/introspection/generated.agi.ts src/introspection/generated.node.ts src/introspection/generated.web.ts assistants/example/CORE.md assistants/example/docs/about.md assistants/example/hooks.ts assistants/example/tools.ts)

Bash(git -C /Users/jon/@luca commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
implement Assistant feature for AGI container

The Assistant feature lets you define an assistant as a folder containing:
- CORE.md: system prompt
- tools.ts: tool implementations with Zod schemas, executed via VM
- hooks.ts: event handler functions bound to assistant lifecycle events
- docs/: structured markdown queried via a DocsReader instance

Each assistant wraps a Conversation and auto-wires streaming events,
tool calls, and hook functions. Includes researchInternalDocs tool
backed by ContentDb for querying internal docs. Supports prepend/append
prompt options and tool/schema overrides via constructor options.

Also adds an example assistant in assistants/example/ and regenerates
introspection metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C /Users/jon/@luca commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
register DocsReader in AGI container

The DocsReader module was only imported as a type, so it was never
registered in the features registry at runtime. Import it as a value
and .use\(\) it so that assistant.initDocsReader\(\) can look it up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C /Users/jon/@luca commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
throw helpful error when looking up unregistered helpers

Registry.lookup\(\) previously returned undefined silently when a helper
wasn''t registered, causing a confusing "undefined is not a constructor"
error downstream. Now it throws immediately with:
- The name of what wasn''t found
- A list of what IS available
- Advice on how to fix it \(import the module or .use\(\) it\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C /Users/jon/@luca commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
use container.fs and container.paths instead of raw Node fs/path

Replace direct imports of readFileSync, existsSync from ''fs'' and
resolve, join from ''path'' with container.fs.readFile\(\),
container.fs.exists\(\), and container.paths.resolve\(\). This keeps the
assistant consistent with the rest of the codebase where all I/O goes
through the container''s helpers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(lane" to "go deeper when needed" to reflect the new capability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
